text.title: Prefix stability is required for the stack to fire
text.summary: Cache hits require byte-identical prefixes across batch items; common drift sources (timestamps, sorted-by-runtime tools, randomized example order) silently break stacking and turn the discount into a tax.

section.title: Drift sources that kill the cache
section.p: A single byte difference between two items' system+tools section makes the second item a cache miss — it pays 1.25× cache-write again. Across a 100k-item batch this is the difference between a 5% input cost and a 125% input cost. Every drift source below is a real production failure in pipelines that "should have stacked".

rule.statement: Build the cached prefix once OUTSIDE the per-item loop, freeze its bytes (no f-strings, no time.time(), no dict-iteration order), and assert that hashlib.sha256(prefix) is constant across items before submitting the batch.
rule.rationale: Anthropic and Gemini both key cache lookups on exact prefix bytes including whitespace and tool ordering; an automated hash check at submit time catches drift that humans miss in code review.

example.code: # Three drift sources in five lines for doc in docs: sys = f"You are an analyst. Today is {date.today()}. Doc: {doc.id}" # 1 tools = sorted(TOOL_DICT.values(), key=lambda t: t.last_used) # 2 examples = random.sample(EXAMPLES, 3) # 3 submit({"system": sys, "tools": tools, "messages": [...]})
example.note: Date in the system prompt, runtime-sorted tools, and randomized examples each independently invalidate the cache. The pipeline pays cache-write tax on EVERY item.

example.code:
SYSTEM = "You are an analyst. Follow the schema below." # frozen TOOLS = list(TOOL_DICT.values()) # insertion-ordered PREFIX_HASH = hashlib.sha256( json.dumps({"sys": SYSTEM, "tools": TOOLS}, sort_keys=True).encode() ).hexdigest()
for doc in docs: item = {"system": [{"type": "text", "text": SYSTEM, "cache_control": {"type": "ephemeral"}}], "tools": TOOLS, "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": doc.text}]} assert PREFIX_HASH == _hash_prefix(item) # cheap guard submit(item)
example.note: Prefix is built once and reused; the per-item hash check fails fast if any future refactor reintroduces drift.

antipattern.description: Putting the per-item context (URL, doc text, user question) at the START of the messages section instead of as a regular `user` turn at the end.
antipattern.reason: Cache hits require the variable content to come AFTER the cache_control breakpoint; fronting it makes every item miss — same failure mode as drift, harder to spot.

section.reference: Reference layout: cached prefix frozen at module load, variable content only in the user message.
